text.title: Backend Languages Practices Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the practices-backend-languages methodology. Each pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Go projects MUST use cmd/internal/pkg layout; errors MUST wrap with fmt.Errorf("context: %w", err); interfaces MUST be defined in the consumer package; concurrency MUST use worker-pool with buffered channels.
rule.rationale: cmd/internal/pkg is the de facto community layout; %w enables errors.Is/As; consumer-side interfaces follow Go's implicit-satisfaction model and prevent import cycles; worker pools prevent goroutine leaks.

rule.statement: Rails controllers MUST handle only HTTP. Business logic MUST live in service objects; complex validations in form objects; complex queries in query objects; view logic in presenters.
rule.rationale: Fat controllers and fat models are untestable without the full Rails stack; single-responsibility objects can be unit-tested in isolation.

rule.statement: Laravel controllers MUST delegate business logic to a Service class injected through the constructor; controllers MUST use Form Requests for validation.
rule.rationale: Same separation-of-concerns logic as Rails; Laravel's service container makes constructor DI trivial.

rule.statement: Spring Boot services MUST use constructor injection (final fields + @RequiredArgsConstructor or explicit constructor). Field @Autowired is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Constructor injection makes dependencies explicit and lets unit tests pass mocks without spinning a Spring context.

rule.statement: C# .NET services MUST implement an IService interface and be injected by interface; all I/O-bound methods MUST be async Task<T>; synchronous I/O inside an async method is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Interface-based DI enables mocking; sync I/O inside async causes deadlocks in ASP.NET request contexts.

rule.statement: Rust projects MUST use thiserror for error types and return Result<T, E> from all fallible functions; .unwrap() / panic! are forbidden in library code; async MUST use tokio; blocking I/O inside an async fn MUST go through tokio::task::spawn_blocking.
rule.rationale: Result types make failures explicit; thiserror generates Display/From impls; blocking I/O inside #[tokio::main] freezes the runtime and deadlocks under load.
